title: Bug in SELECT DISTINCT Query with Complex JOIN Condition: Missing Expected Results
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1(id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL);
CREATE TABLE t2(t1_id INT, value FLOAT, FOREIGN KEY (t1_id) REFERENCES t1(id));
INSERT INTO t2(t1_id, value) VALUES (1, 0.5), (2, 1.5), (3, NULL);
SELECT DISTINCT t1.name FROM t1 JOIN t2 ON t1.id = t2.t1_id WHERE t2.value IS NOT NULL;
key_bug_features: SELECT DISTINCT with complex JOIN condition fails to return results
